description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly gray coloration interspersed with white marbling and patches. The surface is highly textured, featuring both smooth and jagged areas, with some deep crevices and pitted sections visible, especially on one side. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible fractures or signs of artificial modification. Its coloration suggests a composition similar to granite or marble, with a mix of lighter and darker mineral inclusions. The rock's overall form is asymmetrical, with one side more flattened and the other more bulbous and rough. This type of rock could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, gray-and-white marbled rock with an irregular, angular shape and a highly textured, rough surface. It features deep crevices and a mix of smooth and jagged areas.
description_view_0: This view shows a relatively flat, broad surface with prominent white marbling and subtle gray veins, giving the rock a somewhat smooth but uneven appearance.
description_view_1: Here, the rock's surface appears more angular and sloped, with a mix of gray and white patches and a slightly sharper edge visible along the bottom.
description_view_2: This side reveals a rough, bulbous section with deep crevices and a more pitted texture, dominated by a mottled gray and white pattern.
description_view_3: The rock's most rugged face is visible, featuring pronounced indentations, jagged protrusions, and a darker, more shadowed appearance.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside area
